title: Can a Fractional CMO Help My Struggling Marketing Strategy? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: Your marketing probably isn't struggling. It's working as designed, reproducing the customers you already have. Sometimes the problem isn't even marketing. How a fractional CMO tells the difference. Daniel Fox, fractional B2C CMO.

mainEntity.name: Can a fractional CMO help my struggling marketing strategy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Often, but first by reframing what struggling means. When a number stops responding to effort across more than one capable team, that is usually an answers problem, not an execution problem. Your marketing is not broken. It is working as designed, reproducing the customers you already have, and the answers that produced your revenue also produced your ceiling. A fractional CMO can help if the gap is strategic judgment: deciding who the customer is and what to say. But not everything is even a marketing problem. The real cause may be product-market fit waning, slow turnaround times, worse reviews, a fading trending product, or a channel under-reporting traffic. A real CMO rules those out before selling a marketing fix. Daniel Fox is a fractional B2C CMO who does that diagnosis first.

@graph.headline: Can a fractional CMO help my struggling marketing strategy?
@graph.description: Struggling marketing is usually working as designed, reproducing the customers you already have. How to tell an answers problem from an execution problem, and when the issue is not marketing at all.
